fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Minnesota Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Minnesota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity minnesota, how to transfer physical therapist license to minnesota, physical therapist compact minnesota, minnesota physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Minnesota Board of Medical Practice — PT
board.mailing_address:
Minnesota Board of Physical Therapy
335 Randolph Avenue, Suite 170
St. Paul, MN 55102

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Minnesota.

steps.title: Create an Account and Start Application
steps.description: Register for a new user account on the Minnesota Online Services portal. Once registered and logged in, select 'Apply for a New License' and choose 'Physical Therapist by Endorsement'.

steps.title: Complete Online Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out the online application completely, providing all required personal and professional information, and submit the application with the initial application fee through the Minnesota Online Services portal.

steps.title: Request Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your physical therapy educational institution to send your official academic transcripts directly to the Minnesota Board of Physical Therapy.

steps.title: Submit NBPTSE Scores
steps.description: Request the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT) to send your official National Board for Certification in Physical Therapy (NBPTSE) scores directly to the Minnesota Board of Physical Therapy.

steps.title: Provide Licensure Verifications
steps.description: Complete the 'Verification of Licensure' form (available on the Board's website) and send it to all states where you currently hold or have previously held a PT license, instructing them to send the completed verification directly to the Minnesota Board of Physical Therapy.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Minnesota physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $100. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Minnesota have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Minnesota participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Minnesota without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Minnesota?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Minnesota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Minnesota
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Minnesota, you must use the Minnesota Online Services portal, which handles all new applications and renewals.

variants.label: New Applicant

variants.label: Reciprocity

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Minnesota Online Services portal, click 'New User Registration' or 'Create Account', and follow the prompts to set up your user profile.
